'''''Star Wars'' 6''' is the sixth issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Roy Thomas]], illustrated by [[Howard Chaykin]], and first published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[September 13]], [[1977]]. It features the story "'''The Final Chapter?'''", which adapts the final scenes from the film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].

==Publisher's summary==
''The conclusion of the adaptation of ''A New Hope''! Can the Rebels destroy the Death Star?''

==Plot summary==
The [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] imminent attack on the Rebel Alliance [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] prompts the Rebels to send a squad of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] to destroy the giant [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. A [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]] ensues between the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], with the timely help of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], exploits the Death Star's tiny weakness and destroys it, saving the day for the Rebellion. On Yavin 4, an [[Royal Award Ceremony/Legends|award ceremony]] is held for the Rebel heroes.

==Continuity==
As with [[Star Wars (1977) 5|issue 5]], [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] is identified as [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]].<ref name="Star Wars 5">[[Star Wars (1977) 5|''Star Wars'' (1977) 5]]</ref>

''Star Wars'' 6 marked the first instance of [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Jek Tono Porkins/Legends|Porkins']] first name, Tono. However, the [[1989]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' later gave Porkins the first name Jek.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 1">''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]''</ref> This discrepancy was [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] in the [[2002]] article "[[Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of Star Wars|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of ''Star Wars'']]," which gave his full name as Jek Tono Porkins.<ref name="GamerCite">{{GamerCite|9|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of Star Wars|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of ''Star Wars''}}</ref>

The cover art depicts Luke and Darth Vader fighting in the presence of Leia. Luke and Vader never meet face-to-face in ''A New Hope''.
